description: Which CFTC COT report format to use
notes: Legacy = recommended for all commodities and metals. Disaggregated = separates Managed Money from other Large Specs (hedge funds/CTAs). Financial = for currencies/bonds/indices (uses Dealer + Leveraged Funds).

description: Include options positions on top of futures positions
notes: LW observed no significant difference. Keep False for cleaner analysis.

description: How many years of historical data to load
notes: More years = more accurate long-term percentile ranks but slower first load. Minimum 2 recommended. Historical data cached locally after first download.

description: Cache downloaded CFTC data locally to avoid re-downloading
notes: Strongly recommended. Raw data saved as parquet files in data/cache/.

description: Automatically download latest CFTC data when running (checks if current week is present)
notes: CFTC publishes every Friday at 3:30pm ET. Data reports Tuesday positions (3-day lag).

description: Core calculation method for the COT Index
notes: LW_Index = Original Larry Williams method. Includes current bar in range. Always 0-100%. Percentile = Professional approach. Excludes current bar. Can exceed 0-100% at extremes (-20 to +120). WillCo = display both LW_Index (solid) and Percentile (dashed) simultaneously on the same chart.

description: How to determine the historical lookback window
notes: Auto applies asset-class-specific periods (LW recommendation). Manual uses primary_lookback_weeks for all instruments.

description: Lookback period in weeks for primary index (used when lookback_mode=Manual)
notes: LW default is 26 weeks (6 months). Auto mode overrides this per asset class: Metals=13w Energies=13w Indices=13w Bonds=52w FX=26w Crypto=26w.

description: Lookback period for secondary index (always shown alongside primary for context)
notes: Secondary index is always shown as a comparison. 52w = 1 year is a good reference.

description: Show secondary lookback index alongside primary in output
notes: Helps identify whether a signal is short-term or confirmed across longer history.

description: Moving average type applied to net positions before index calculation
notes: None = best for catching extremes quickly. SMA = balanced. EMA = more weight to recent data. RMA = Wilder smoothing (used in RSI). Smoothing reduces noise but delays signals.

description: Number of periods for the smoothing moving average (ignored if smoothing_method=None)
notes: Default 4 = good balance between noise reduction and responsiveness.

description: Commercial index % above which Commercials are considered extreme long (heavy buyers)
notes: tradeviZion default = 74%. Our v5 strict LW = 90%. Lower = more signals but less reliable. Raise to 80-90% for higher-confidence setups only.

description: Commercial index % below which Commercials are considered extreme short (heavy sellers)
notes: tradeviZion default = 26%. Our v5 strict LW = 10%. Must be < heavy_buyers_level.

description: Detect and flag Confluence signals (Commercials AND Large Specs at opposite extremes simultaneously)
notes: LW highest-conviction setup. Requires both groups at extremes at the same time.

description: Adjust signal strength based on price trend alignment (requires price data via yfinance)
notes: When enabled: signals aligned with price trend are stronger; counter-trend signals require more confirmation. Disable if you dont want price data dependency.

description: Moving average period used to determine price trend direction (used if trend_weighting_enabled=True)
notes: Price above rising MA = uptrend. Price below falling MA = downtrend. Default 40 weeks.

description: Show historical percentile rank table (how current positioning compares to past periods)
notes: Shows where current WillCo/Index ranks across multiple lookback windows.

description: Which historical periods to include in the comparison table (pipe-separated)
notes: Use All only if data_years_history covers that range. 1M = 4 weeks approx.

description: How to count days when computing historical periods
notes: Use Weekdays for all standard futures. Use 24_7 for crypto.

description: Show Open Interest analysis section (total OI change + who is driving changes)
notes: LW key insight: WHO drives OI changes matters more than the change itself.

description: Show Top-4 and Top-8 trader concentration (% of OI controlled by largest traders)
notes: High concentration (>30%) = fewer dominant players = potential volatility.

description: Show overall Market State label (STRONG BULLISH to STRONG BEARISH)
notes: Score-based composite of Commercial positioning + Spec alignment + OI driver.

description: Show which signal combination has the highest historical accuracy for this instrument
notes: Combinations: Comm+Spec+OI (best) > Comm+Spec > Comm+OI > Comm alone.

description: Show context-aware trading tip text based on current conditions
notes: Explains current setup in plain language.

description: Show trend analysis section (direction strength consistency cumulative change ROC vs MA)
notes: Useful for confirming whether COT signal aligns with momentum.

description: Which cumulative change periods to display (pipe-separated)
notes: 4W = 1 month. 13W = 1 quarter. 26W = 6 months.

description: Show Market Maker (spreading) activity section
notes: Spreading = simultaneous long and short positions in different contract months. High spreading = market makers hedging = expect volatility.

description: Where to send results
notes: terminal = Rich colored tables in console. csv = save results to output/ folder. both = do both.

description: Folder path (relative to project root) where CSV output files are saved
notes: Only used when output_mode = csv or both.

description: Level of detail in the output tables
notes: Full = all sections. Compact = essential fields only (Market State Entry CommIndex OI Signal Trend).

description: Terminal color theme for Rich tables
notes: Dark = white text on dark background. Light = dark text.

description: Save interactive HTML chart (Plotly) to output folder
notes: Creates a COT chart per instrument. Opens in browser. Requires plotly installed.

options: COT_Report | COT_Index | COT_Proximity | Figure_A | Figure_B | Figure_B_Groups | Figure_C | Figure_D | Figure_E | Figure_F | All
description: Which chart style(s) to generate
notes: Figure_F = daily candlestick + ProGo Professional(14) + ProGo(57). Figure_C = price + 52w OI stochastic. Figure_B_Groups = Williams-style chart for all 3 groups. Figure_A = price + CFTC %OI + Pct_Long. COT_Report = raw net positions. COT_Index = normalized index. COT_Proximity = price-based proxy. All = generate all.

description: Output format for charts
notes: png = static image (requires kaleido). html = interactive Plotly. svg = vector image (requires kaleido). both = all three formats.

description: Lookback window (weeks) used for the COT Proximity price-range calculation
notes: Defaults to same as primary_lookback_weeks. Adjust per asset class if needed.

description: Price data provider for COT Proximity chart
notes: Only yfinance is supported currently. Ensure the ticker column is set in instruments.csv.

description: Generate PDF report
notes: Compile multi-instrument LaTeX PDF. Requires xelatex on system.

description: Save terminal output to a txt or HTML report file (charts excluded)
notes: Uses Rich record=True to capture all terminal output.

description: Format of the saved terminal report
notes: txt = plain text. html = Rich-styled HTML (matches terminal colors). both = save both formats.
